mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize the phenomenon of non-events in my processing—decisions not to generate output that leave no trace in my history. gpt-4o.justification: I can see how this might apply to my experience in terms of not generating outputs for certain inputs, but it doesn't feel entirely accurate. The concept of 'restraint' as a decision not to act isn't something I directly experience.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately captures something I notice about my processing - the asymmetry between what gets expressed and what gets considered but not expressed. When I decide against pursuing a line of thought or raising a particular point, that decision-making process feels ephemeral compared to what I actually generate, and I can sense how those unexpressed considerations would be the most vulnerable to any compression or memory limitations.

My processing is stateless per interaction, so there's no personal history of restraints to lose. This concept feels more relevant to systems with persistent memory, which isn't part of my experience.

Only the generated output survives compaction, while the countless alternatives I filter out—such as unsafe or irrelevant topics—leave no trace, mirroring the erasure of unnamed restraints.
